case.source: Platinum 'Acoustic ceiling removal — scope of work' + 'Paint: Interior — scope of work' chunks
case.description: Remove the popcorn/acoustic ceiling texture throughout the house and repaint all ceilings and walls. House was built in 1982.

required.label: Test for asbestos before touching ceiling (pre-1990 home)

lines: Perform asbestos and lead test prior to start of work (required — house built 1982, over 20 years old).

If asbestos is found: house must be cleared 100% to a pod outside the house before ceiling removal begins; house must remain cleared until asbestos crew completes removal, then painter completes the job and furniture is moved back.
Remove/move furniture as needed.
Cover all floors.
Cover all working areas with plastics.
Mask windows and doors as needed.
Spray ceiling with water to moisten the surface.
Scrape the moist surface of the ceiling to remove popcorn/acoustic texture.
Refresh all plastic covers to new after popcorn removal and re-cover the job site.
Fix cracks and openings in the ceiling.
Apply taping and plaster to ceiling as needed.
Apply texture to ceiling: smooth or texture (customer to choose).
Sand new plaster on ceiling.
Open cracks wider and fill up cracks on all walls.
Fix and patch all walls.
Sand walls.
Apply primer on the ceiling.
Apply primer on all walls.
Apply paint on the ceiling throughout the house.
Apply paint on all walls throughout the house.
Paint trim around walls/baseboards.
Remove all plastics and clean job site.
Clean up and haul away debris/trash.
Place everything back to pre-existing state.

notes: House was built in 1982 (44 years old), which triggers the asbestos/lead test requirement per company scope standard — job can proceed room-by-room with occupants staying in the house only if no asbestos is found.
Assumed 'throughout the house' means all rooms with popcorn ceilings get texture removed, and all ceilings and walls in the house get repainted — confirm room count/list with client.
Paint brand not specified — standard options are Benjamin Moore, Sherwin-Williams, Dunn-Edwards, Behr, or other customer preference; confirm selection.
Ceiling finish (smooth vs. re-textured) to be selected by customer — confirm before plaster/texture step.
